When a key is active, when the user hovers over a different key, the active tooltip disappears.

The tooltip of the key that the user is hovering on appears.

11208:326801.name: Similar to mouse, DDPM will have a button similar to mouse app-specific section 'Restore to All Applications' individually for apps in app panel. Clicking on this button will revert the app-specific action for that particular app back to Global assignment actions, e.g. M1 has: Global custom action = Volume mute Teams action = Play/Pause multimedia MS word action = insert comment
11208:326801.text_content:
Similar to mouse, DDPM will have a button similar to mouse app-specific section 'Restore to All Applications' individually for apps in app panel. Clicking on this button will revert the app-specific action for that particular app back to Global assignment actions, e.g. M1 has:
Global custom action = Volume mute
Teams action = Play/Pause multimedia
MS word action = insert comment

11208:326803.name: Similar to mouse, DDPM will have a button similar to mouse app-specific section 'Restore to All Applications' individually for apps in app panel. Clicking on this button will revert the app-specific action for that particular app back to Global assignment actions, e.g. M1 has: Global custom action = Volume mute Teams action = Play/Pause multimedia MS word action = insert comment
11208:326803.text_content:
Similar to mouse, DDPM will have a button similar to mouse app-specific section 'Restore to All Applications' individually for apps in app panel. Clicking on this button will revert the app-specific action for that particular app back to Global assignment actions, e.g. M1 has:
Global custom action = Volume mute
Teams action = Play/Pause multimedia
MS word action = insert comment

11208:326805.name: The user customizes the M1 key to perform "Volume Mute" action under the "All Applications" setting. Later, the user enters application-specific setting, M1 will follow the previous customized setting under “All Application”. “Restore to all application” button will be in disabled state.
11208:326805.text_content: The user customizes the M1 key to perform "Volume Mute" action under the "All Applications" setting. Later, the user enters application-specific setting, M1 will follow the previous customized setting under “All Application”. “Restore to all application” button will be in disabled state.

11208:326807.name: Displays up to 5 apps. If more than 5, an arrow will appear to indicate additional items.
11208:326807.text_content: Displays up to 5 apps. If more than 5, an arrow will appear to indicate additional items.

11208:326809.name: The user customizes the M1 key to perform "Volume Mute" action under the "All Applications" setting. Later, the user enters application-specific setting and customizes the M1 key to perform “Screen Snip”, M1 will follow the previous customized setting under “All Application”. “Restore to all application” button will be in active state.
11208:326809.text_content: The user customizes the M1 key to perform "Volume Mute" action under the "All Applications" setting. Later, the user enters application-specific setting and customizes the M1 key to perform “Screen Snip”, M1 will follow the previous customized setting under “All Application”. “Restore to all application” button will be in active state.

11208:328838.name: Displays a maximum of 5 application icons in view at once. Additional apps are accessible via horizontal scrolling. Right Arrow: Visible when there are more than 5 apps. Active by default. Greys out when the user scrolls to the far right (end of the list). Left Arrow: Hidden or greyed out when at the far left (start of the list). Becomes active once the user scrolls right.
11208:328838.text_content:
Displays a maximum of 5 application icons in view at once.
Additional apps are accessible via horizontal scrolling.
Right Arrow:
Visible when there are more than 5 apps.
Active by default.
Greys out when the user scrolls to the far right (end of the list).
Left Arrow:
Hidden or greyed out when at the far left (start of the list).
Becomes active once the user scrolls right.

11208:328840.name: Windows Narrator String Guidance All Apps - “Assign Macro Key Customization for all applications” First Individual Apps -”Application list for Macro Key Customization” Left Arrow Active - “Show more applications to the left” Left Arrow Inactive - If an arrow is inactive, should Windows Narrator read it? If yes, maybe it should read something like “There are no more applications in this list in this direction” Right Arrow Active - “Show more applications to the right” Right Arrow Inactive - If an arrow is inactive, should Windows Narrator read it? If yes, maybe it should read something like “There are no more applications in the list in this direction” + icon - Add new application for use with Macro Key Customization
11208:328840.text_content:
Windows Narrator String Guidance
All Apps - “Assign Macro Key Customization for all applications”
First Individual Apps -”Application list for Macro Key Customization” 
Left Arrow Active -  “Show more applications to the left”
Left Arrow Inactive - If an arrow is inactive, should Windows Narrator read it? If yes, maybe it should read something like “There are no more applications in this list in this direction”
Right Arrow Active -  “Show more applications to the right” 
Right Arrow Inactive - If an arrow is inactive, should Windows Narrator read it? If yes, maybe it should read something like “There are no more applications in the list in this direction”
+ icon - Add new application for use with Macro Key Customization

11208:317702.layer_name: Assigned Action Flow 1 (Editing a key that has a default action)

11208:317755.name: User lands on device overview page by default with nothing being selected. All keys, except for F2, have default actions. F2 has a non-default assigned action. The navigation tabs are expanded by default here (text + icon)
11208:317755.text_content:
User lands on device overview page by default with nothing being selected.
All keys, except for F2, have default actions. F2 has a non-default assigned action.
The navigation tabs are expanded by default here (text + icon)

11208:317757.name: User hover on ‘F1’ and sees “Volume Mute,” which is the default F1 Action.
11208:317757.text_content: User hover on ‘F1’ and sees “Volume Mute,” which is the default F1 Action.

11208:317759.name: ‘F1’ has the default action assigned “Multimedia Actions” is automatically expanded because the current F1 action is in that section. Because “Volume Mute” is the default action for F1, the “Remove” button is hidden. ‘F2’ is on assigned-default state, which means the user has already assigned an action to it. Ways to pull out keyboard setting sheet: (1). Click on the available keys (F1: on default-selected state). (2). Click on ‘keyboard setting’ tab on the side menu bar.
11208:317759.text_content:
‘F1’ has the default action assigned
“Multimedia Actions” is automatically expanded because the current F1 action is in that section.
Because “Volume Mute” is the default action for F1, the “Remove” button is hidden.
‘F2’ is on assigned-default state, which means the user has already assigned an action to it.
Ways to pull out keyboard setting sheet:
      (1). Click on the available keys (F1: on default-selected state).
      (2). Click on ‘keyboard setting’ tab on the side menu bar.

11208:317761.name: User clicks on the search bar and types in the action that they want to assign to ‘F1’ The most relevant results appear in a box called “Search Results.”
11208:317761.text_content:
User clicks on the search bar and types in the action that they want to assign to ‘F1’
The most relevant results appear in a box called “Search Results.”

11208:317763.name: If the action that they want shows up, they can click on the search result to assign it to ‘F1’ ‘Copy’ is now assigned to ‘F1’ which changes it to active-default state.
11208:317763.text_content:
If the action that they want shows up, they can click on the search result to assign it to ‘F1’
‘Copy’ is now assigned to ‘F1’ which changes it to active-default state.

11208:317765.name: User clicks on “F2” which has an action assigned If the user clicks “Edit” then they can edit the keystroke.
11208:317765.text_content:
User clicks on “F2” which has an action assigned
If the user clicks “Edit” then they can edit the keystroke.

11208:317855.layer_name: Assigned Action Flow 2 (Editing a key that has an assigned action)

11208:317907.name: User lands on device overview page by default with nothing being selected. All keys, except for F1 and F2, have default actions. F1 and F2 have a non-default assigned action. The navigation tabs are expanded by default here (text + icon)
11208:317907.text_content:
User lands on device overview page by default with nothing being selected.
All keys, except for F1 and F2, have default actions. F1 and F2 have a non-default assigned action.
The navigation tabs are expanded by default here (text + icon)

11208:317909.name: User hover on ‘F1’ and sees “Documents,” which is NOT the default F1 Action.
11208:317909.text_content: User hover on ‘F1’ and sees “Documents,” which is NOT the default F1 Action.

11208:317911.name: ‘F1’ has a non-default action assigned (Documents) “Productivity Actions” is automatically expanded because the current assigned F1 action is in that section. Because the current assigned action (Documents) is not the default “F1” action, the “Remove” button is shown.
11208:317911.text_content:
‘F1’ has a non-default action assigned (Documents)
“Productivity Actions” is automatically expanded because the current assigned F1 action is in that section.
Because the current assigned action (Documents) is not the default “F1” action, the “Remove” button is shown.

11208:317913.name: After the user clicks on “Remove,” the F1 action returns to the default F1 action, which is “Volume Mute.” The “Multimedia Actions” menu will expand automatically. The key highlight changes to the “active” state.
11208:317913.text_content:
After the user clicks on “Remove,” the F1 action returns to the default F1 action, which is “Volume Mute.”
The “Multimedia Actions” menu will expand automatically.
The key highlight changes to the “active” state.

11208:115688.name: GIVEN: Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK DDPM 1.7.1 is on the system AND Collaboration Icons is turned OFF (If Zoom is not installed, please note that in DDPM, by default, icons are greyed out OFF and the user cannot toggle) AND Zoom is not on the system THEN: Show this error: "To use Collaboration Keyboard you need the latest version of Zoom" Follow the styling according to the figma designs Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button
11208:115688.text_content:
GIVEN:
Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK
DDPM 1.7.1 is on the system AND
Collaboration Icons is turned OFF (If Zoom is not installed, please note that in DDPM, by default, icons are greyed out OFF and the user cannot toggle) AND
Zoom is not on the system
THEN:
Show this error:
"To use Collaboration Keyboard you need the latest version of Zoom"
Follow the styling according to the figma designs
Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module
Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button

11208:115693.name: GIVEN: Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK DDPM 1.7.1 is on the system AND Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND Zoom is on the system and out of date THEN: Show this error: "To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version" Follow the styling according to the figma designs Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button
11208:115693.text_content:
GIVEN:
Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK
DDPM 1.7.1 is on the system AND
Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND
Zoom is on the system and out of date
THEN:
Show this error:
"To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version"
Follow the styling according to the figma designs
Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module
Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button

11208:115698.name: GIVEN: Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK DDPM 2.3.0 is on the system AND Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND Zoom is on the system and out of date AND THEN: Show error: "To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version" Follow the styling according to the figma designs Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button
11208:115698.text_content:
GIVEN:
Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK
DDPM 2.3.0 is on the system AND
Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND
Zoom is on the system and out of date AND
THEN:
Show error:
"To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version"
Follow the styling according to the figma designs
Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module
Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button

11208:115702.name: GIVEN: Collaboration Keyboard/Icons is turned OFF either by user or Locked OFF by IT Admin THEN: Do not show any errors WHEN: The user or IT Admin turns Collaboration Keyboard/Icons ON Immediately surface any applicable errors
11208:115702.text_content:
GIVEN:
Collaboration Keyboard/Icons is turned OFF either by user or Locked OFF by IT Admin
THEN:
Do not show any errors
WHEN:
The user or IT Admin turns Collaboration Keyboard/Icons ON
Immediately surface any applicable errors

11208:115707.name: GIVEN: Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK DDPM 2.3.0 is on the system AND Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND Zoom is on the system and out of date THEN: Show error: "To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version" Follow the styling according to the figma designs Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button
11208:115707.text_content:
GIVEN:
Commercial Platform supports DDPM and CTK
DDPM 2.3.0 is on the system AND
Collaboration Icons is turned ON AND
Zoom is on the system and out of date
THEN:
Show error:
"To use Collaboration Keyboard with Zoom, install Zoom’s latest desktop version"
Follow the styling according to the figma designs
Show the error inside the feature card instead of at the top of the module
Allow the user to dismiss this error by hitting the "x" button

11208:343209.name: Copy
11208:343209.text_content: Select “All” to customize your Macro Keys regardless of the app being used. You may also choose the plus icon to add specific apps and create shortcuts for when they are in use.

11208:343252.name: Copy
11208:343252.text_content: You may also assign actions to many of the keys in the top row of your keyboard. Note: Only your Macro Keys can be assigned different actions per application.

11208:343391.text_content: Customize your Macro Keys by inputting your own keystrokes or choose an action from the listed categories

11208:343448.name: Copy
11208:343448.text_content: Select “All” to customize your Macro Keys regardless of the app being used. You may also choose the plus icon to add specific apps and create shortcuts for when they are in use.

11208:343491.name: Copy
11208:343491.text_content: You may also assign actions to many of the keys in the top row of your keyboard. Note: Only your Macro Keys can be assigned different actions per application.

11208:343630.name: Copy
11208:343630.text_content: Customize your Macro Keys by inputting your own keystrokes or choose an action from the listed categories

11208:343663.name: Copy
11208:343663.text_content: You can customize the keys on your keyboard by assigning your favorite actions to the top row keys
